# icotera-i4850_port_forward (Resource)

IPv4 Port Forwarding

This resource sets up port forwarding rules that forward external requests to a particular port (or range of ports) to a single port on a device inside the network.

The provider adds entries to the bottom of the port forward list in the router web interface, which visually separates automatic entries from any manually administered entries at the top of the page. The provider will not overwrite any enabled manual entries.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "icotera-i4850_port_forward" "example1" {
  name = "example1"
  # protocol can be "tcp", "udp", or "Both"
  protocol = "udp"
  external_port_start = "5154"
  # You don't need to specify external_port_end if you only want to forward a single port
  # If forwarding a range of external ports, they are all sent to a single internal port
  external_port_end = "5154" 
  internal_ip = "192.168.15.16"
  internal_port = "5155"
  loopback = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `external_port_start` (Number) The lowest (or only) port in the external port range
- `internal_ip` (String) The internal IPv4 address that incoming connections will be forwarded to
- `internal_port` (Number) The internal port that external connections will be forwarded to
- `name` (String) A name for the forwarding rule
- `protocol` (String) tcp, udp, or both

### Optional

- `external_port_end` (Number) The highest port of the external port range (can be left out when forwarding single ports)
- `loopback` (Boolean) Whether to also forward the port for requests from inside the network (so you could access a service via the external ip address whether inside or outside the network)

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The 1-128 slot index on the router.
